lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that cute creature wandering around?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oh Izzie? He's my pet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of creature is Izzie?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I'm not sure. He's very cute though don't you think? It's very restful having a pet, especially when you are stuck in a cave for as long as I am!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what do you do down here anyway?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Isn't that obvious? I investigate Thaumaturgy, I research the Arcane, I attempt to explain the inexplicable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You study magic then, what kind of magic? I do a bit of magic myself you know.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Are you a budding mage? I can tell you more about my research if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that sounds fascinating...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Excellent, it's so rare I can find someone to discuss my researches, most gnomes seem to find it all very tedious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never. How could they think that? Though, I suppose gnomes aren't known for their magic.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well that's true. These days, apart from a few combat spells most gnomes seem to be a bit suspicious of mages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Why is that?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well there is a bit of history there, do you want me to tell you about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yes I love a bit of History.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Very wise, a good knowledge of the History of Magic is crucial to understanding the Arcane.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, the most famous Gnomish Mage of all got into a bit of .... trouble.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Most gnomes don't really talk about him. Other than telling stories about him as if he was some sort of boogie-man.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Ridiculous! He was a gnome like any other - he sounds like a fascinating character.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: He was one of King Healthorg the Great's advisors during the Grand Migration. But he was exiled after the Goblin Wars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why was that?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, I'm not really sure. This was all before I was born - and no-one talks about what happened to Glouphrie these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He must have done something very bad then.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, maybe? I'm not so sure. As it happens I've been looking into that part of our History. All I've managed to establish was that he was particularly interested in optics and light magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's hard to see what he could misuse light in a way that would get him exiled.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, indeed. I have also found though he was generally disliked by other gnomes - his moniker was 'Glouphrie the Untrusted'.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oaknock the Engineer and Glouphrie didn't like each other either...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oaknock? I haven't heard of him before.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Ah, Oaknock was an engineer and architect. Very famous. There are statues to him around the place - his reputation amongst gnomes is second only to King Healthorg himself. He built most of the Stronghold you know.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Are you really interested in all of this? Do you want to see something interesting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, have a careful look around at everything in the next cave and come back and tell me what you find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errm, not right now. I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Fine! Humans really do prefer to live their lives in ignorance...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm more interested in your researches right now.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Gah! Young mages these days. No patience. No perseverance. Magic is not a toy or a shiny weapon you know! You cannot alter reality unless you understand it. And you cannot understand unless you search for the true causes of things. And history is a record of the causes of things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I stand corrected - tell me more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I'd better get going...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, I'd better carry on with my research then..

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, I'd better carry on with my research then..

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail again before looking at the room

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We were discussing gnome magic...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Ah, yes indeed. Well I do have a little mystery for you. Has your curiosity been piqued?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, have a careful look around at everything in the next cave and come back and tell me what you find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm a bit busy right now.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Fine, wander blindly on then!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had a look in the other room now.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So? What did you find?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There was a pit with a crystalline looking bowl in the centre.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Indeed, sadly I know very little about that, it looks elven to me though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is also a large strange looking machine...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That's right. It was built by Oaknock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know that?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That room used to be Oaknock's workshop. I'd also recognise his style anywhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it do?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I have no idea. But I do know someone who might.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: There is one gnome left who knew Oaknock, and King Healthorg and Glouphrie for that matter...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is that?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Have a guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errm....

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, King Narnode tells me you've already visited him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I know, you're talking about Hazelmere aren't you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Very good, young apprentice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't he help create the Grand Tree?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That's right. He was the third advisor to King Healthorg, he found and tended to the Grand Tree.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: If you are really interested in all of this, then could you do me a favour and visit Hazelmere for me? I've been meaning to go for a while, but I have responsibilities here - there's always someone who wants to use the Rune Essence mines.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, I'd love to!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Good! Good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute, am I going to have to translate everything he says using that dictionary again?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Ha! He's an old fashioned kind of gnome and doesn't like visitors much. But I'm sure he'll want to talk about the Old Days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he only speaks Ancient Gnomish!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That may be true, though he may just be faking it to deter visitors. Either way - he is a wise old gnome. If he really wants to talk to you I'm sure he'll find a way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well alright, if you say so. What do you want me to ask him?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well ask him if he knows anything about Oaknock's machine. And anything you can find about Glouphrie would be very interesting, wouldn't you agree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right then. I'd better be on my way. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm a bit busy right now.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Fine, wander blindly on then!

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail again before going to Hazelmere

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So haven't you managed to have a chat with Hazelmere yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet I'm afraid. Where do I find him again?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: He has a hut in a small island east of Yanille. It has a lovely sea-view, I'm jealous. I love my cave, but it's not very picturesque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you need some pictures. Though your pot plant adds something to ambiance of the place.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oh, that's not my potplant. I think it was Oaknock's.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? It's survived a long time. It must be a special pot plant.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I suppose it must be. It shakes every now and again you know... I don't know why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How very odd. Anyway, I'd better get on my way to Hazelmere... See you soon.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, hello Hazelmere.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah blah human. Sarko!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brimstail has sent me to ask you some questions.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Ha! Atarischeray! Blah blah mesi blah blah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some questions about the old days. Brimstail has been filling me in about Gnomish History.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah Brimstail blah blah human blah minahar!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like a fascinating time. It also sounds like some important things happened then. Events that we shouldn't forget.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmmph. Laquinay blah human. Blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He has also found a machine - he says it was built by Oaknock...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Oaknock! Ha blah blah minapor blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah blah blah Oaknock blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah blah blah blah Healthorg cinqo blah blah blah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, I'm sorry I don't understand a word you're saying.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: *SIGH* Ipmesi human.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: ... ethereal link ... failed ... puny human mind ... insufficiently disciplined ... mental rigour required ... trained ... magical skills ...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmph. Not bad for a human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weird! How are you doing this Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Old magicks beyond your skill human. You can stop flapping your big human lips as well. Just think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do I do this. Wow. That's impressive. Brimstail was telling me that Gnomes don't trust Mages these days. Can you hear me? Glouphrie wasn't it? He did something bad and got exiled. Ooh, I'm feeling a little dizzy. You knew him didn't you? What can you tell me about him? And what's this machine that Oaknock built? Isn't that magical as well? Do you know what it does?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Stop wittering on [player name]. You're giving me a headache. This mind speech is hard enough without having to listen to your incoherent babbling. Think about nothing. Clear your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clear my mind? Hey, how did you know my name.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I'm reading your mind foolish human. How do you think? Now CLEAR YOUR MIND...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I think about nothing? I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm tasty lobster. Gah! I'm thinking about something. Stop thinking about Lobsters. Pink Lobsters. Big Lobsters. Lobsters with wings. Wings? Lobsters don't have wings. ARRGH. I can't do this.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Just shut up and listen! I have a story to tell you that will answer most of your questions.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Let us travel back in time, to the time you humans call the Fourth Age. King Healthorg had become King. The elves mysteriously decided to travel west, leaving most of Kandarin open for conquest. The humans weren't here yet, only King Healthorg and the gnomes to the north, and a collection of Goblin tribes to the south.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Both armies moved to claim the lands, and were of course destined to meet in battle...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: And so it came to pass the Goblins and Gnome armies met at the battle of Atarisundri.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Both sides faced each other on the Battlefield. A bloody battle was in prospect, our gnomes were brave, but we were no match for the mass of brute goblin stupidity...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: ... but Glouphrie and King Healthorg had devised a plan...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: ... If you don't lead an invincible army - pretend you do...

lines.speaker: Glouphrie
lines.text: Em Gal lauf rei!

lines.speaker: Glouphrie
lines.text: Emeto Laufolly egramentos umesco lamiten gan!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: And so, Glouphrie created an army of illusory Giant tortoises, knowing that goblins would be scared of anything bigger than they were.

lines.speaker: Goblin 1 (unnamed)
lines.text: Big Boss didn' say nuffin 'bout de nomiez havin' monsta tortisses!

lines.speaker: Goblin 2 (unnamed)
lines.text: Dey be huges! Tortisses too scary and pointy. We runz away!

lines.speaker: Goblin 3 (unnamed)
lines.text: Scarey tortisses! Runz!

lines.speaker: Goblin 4 (unnamed)
lines.text: Runz away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Was that you next to the tortoise? You look so handsome!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Handsome! Hmph! That was a long time ago...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Anyway, where was I? Oh yes...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Sadly, the golden times were not destined to last for long...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: There was once a very special Spirit Tree in the Stronghold. Silver as a birch, old and wise - I considered him a friend. I called him Argento, his true name was unpronouncable.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: He was the first Spirit Tree I had befriended, and the one I knew the best. I would sometimes travel, visiting and finding other Spirit trees, and during one such trip, I was filled with a great sense of foreboding...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I felt a shadow pass over me, and a tingling in the air, even though the weather was fine and dry.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Then I felt a sudden pain, as if the Anima Mundi was crying out with the loss of a favoured child...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: And I knew that one of the Old Ones had died, and I had lost a friend.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I rushed straight back to the Grand Tree, only to find that no-one had noticed anything, and that Argento was still standing and seemed alive. But I knew something was wrong. He refused to talk to me and his aura was

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: no longer present. Someone had hidden his death. I talked to Oaknock and he was incensed. He immediately blamed Glouphrie, and although Oaknock could be rather irrational when it came to Glouphrie -

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: they never saw eye to eye - I knew he was right this time. Glouphrie had used his magicks to hide the truth from all of us.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Oaknock had made a machine - an anti-illusion machine, with some help from the elves. He prepared it, and summoned the advisors to Argento..

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Why have you summoned us here?

lines.speaker: Oaknock the Engineer
lines.text: You'll see sire. Switch the machine on!

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: What is the meaning of this?

lines.speaker: Oaknock the Engineer
lines.text: The Silver Spirit Tree is dead!

lines.speaker: Oaknock the Engineer
lines.text: Glouphrie has been using his foul arts to mask the truth! What other secrets has he been keeping?

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Explain yourself!

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: I live to serve you sire. I work for the good of us all.

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: How can lying to your fellow gnomes be 'Good'?

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: Some things are best kept secret...

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Even from your King!?

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: Occasionally.

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Enough! How can I trust a gnome who hides things from me?

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: No gnome can blame you for what you do not know.

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: That is NOT how I rule.

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Glouphrie. You are no longer my advisor. Leave this place.

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: Only the naive believe they can rule without someone like me.

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: Your empire will crack and the gnomes will become but a joke.

lines.speaker: King Healthorg
lines.text: Sarko!

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: So be it. I'll be watching.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: And so Glouphrie left and travelled with some companions to a place they called Arposandra. We have heard nothing but rumours about them since...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where's this Arposandra place anyway?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Nobody knows for certain, somewhere rocky I suppose. The rumour is that after founding his new home, Glouphrie used his magicks to shroud the city in mist. Arposandra may not exist at all, however I suspect that

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Glouphrie is still around, one way or another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you think that?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Just call it Gnomish intuition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the machine that Brimstail has found is the one that Oaknock built to stop Glouphrie's illusions. Do you know how it worked?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I never studied it, but I believe it used elven magic - So it must have used crystals of some sort. Crystal discs, yes that sounds familiar. Actually come to think of it I think I may have one lying around. Here take

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: this, maybe it will help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thank you, Hazelmere. You've told me a lot of things I need to ponder for a while.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Very well. Oh and when you see that lazy toad Brimstail, tell him next time he needs information to come himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that. Farewell.

sections.title: Speaking with Hazelmere again if his dialogue is interrupted

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

sections.title: Talking to Hazelmere again before going back to Brimstail

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: What do you want now? Go and talk to Brimstail, that's why you came to see me in the first place...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've visited Hazelmere, he told me all sorts of interesting things.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Ah, thank you my young apprentice. What did he tell you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Well it starts during the Great Migration...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So the machine emits an anti-illusion glamour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glamour? What's that?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Don't you know anything? *sigh* A glamour is a type of enchantment or magical effect.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes it does have such a glamour. It seems to use crystal discs to work in some way.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oh, crystal discs? I may have a few of those in a drawer somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that? Something just moved behind you...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Really? Wait - what's that banging noise?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: What's happened to the machine? It's broken!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, something is afoot. I sense sabotage....

sections.title: Speaking to Brimstail after the sabotage

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The machine is broken, I suspect sabotage...!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: This is intolerable! How could this happen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm confused, it must have happened while we were here talking!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: But we were just standing here, how could someone get past us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did see something odd behind you...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Wait a minute, how did the saboteur know about the machine?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hmmm ... You're the only person I've told about the machine...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Don't look at me like that. The only person I've talked to is Hazelmere.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Surely this couldn't be the work of Hazelmere... That turncoat!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! Wait! None of this makes any sense. There must be another explanation.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Alright. Someone snuck in here without us noticing. How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The intruder must have been invisible.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Right, and how does one make oneself invisible my [boy/girl]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I've seen this before. Karam in the 10th squad used some sort of magic to make himself invisible.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Do you think the 10th squad did this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, but I think our saboteur must have learnt the same magicks. Is Glouphrie still alive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Illusion magic?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Invisibility is a form of illusion magic. So that makes sense...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this was done by Glouphrie? Is he still alive?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Who knows, but he may well have taught the skills to his gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean the gnomes that went with Glouphrie to Arposandra?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Right. And why would they sabotage an anti-illusion device?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because there is an illusion that they want to remain hidden?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Of course. Which makes it imperative that we fix this machine and find out what it is!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Have a look at the machine and assess the damage. I'll hunt around my boxes and see if I can find anything that might help.

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail again without inspecting the machine

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Have you had a look at the damage yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll get right to it.

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail again after inspecting the machine

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So how are your investigations going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not getting anywhere with these 'sticky runes'.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hmmm, which runes would you think are sticky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Runes are runes! None of them are sticky! They're all round and disc-like and definitely non-adhesive.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Open your mind and think laterally. Thinking sideways is an important skill for a mage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, all right. I'll give it a go. Well - air, water and fire and earth runes. There's nothing sticky about them.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I concur.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mind and body runes. Again distinctly non-glue-like in any way.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Keep going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nature and Cosmic runes. Once more distinctly lacking in any gooeyness. Bah! This is a waste of time.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Patience young apprentice. You are on the right path - just think it through and I'm sure it will become obvious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. What do I do with the rune when I find it?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: What did Oaknock do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it looked powdered in the glue.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So there you go. What more do you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm still a bit unsure about getting this glue.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So, what do you know so far?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looked ambery in colour and sticky, smelt of pine and had flecks of silvery stuff in it.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: It smelt of pine and was ambery in colour, that sounds like tree sap. From a coniferous tree probably.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tree sap?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Have you never tried cutting a coniferous tree and watching the sap drip out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err no.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well maybe if you had, you'd know something about tree sap!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do I put the stuff?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hmmm, use your head! What kind of substance is this tree sap?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Is it a solid, a liquid or a gas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see - a liquid I suppose.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So where do you put liquids?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some form of container?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Right! I think you know everything you need now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. I'll go away and think about what you've said.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That's right, use your noddle, exercise your noggin, employ your cranium. Think, think, think!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now you're just confusing me more...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've fixed the machine now!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well done! You're almost as good at fixing things as a gnome! Who'd have thought?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Though I still don't think it's actually activated. There appears to be some sort of lock on the front. I think I know what the discs are for though. There's a slot in the front that looks like it might fit a disc, and a

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: number.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So what do you think the number means?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure yet. Maybe I should put a disc into the slot and see what happens?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That reminds me, I found those crystal discs I mentioned before.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: They are not quite the same as the one Hazelmere gave you, but they look similar in size and weight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They also look as if they might fit in the slots on both of the machines. Maybe I should try it out?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I wonder if the different sizes and shapes mean anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been thinking that as well, I shall ponder it some more.. I'll work it out.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That's the spirit! This is all good training for the mind...

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail again before unlocking the machine

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hmmm. Maybe all of these discs all have different values and you have to choose the right one in order for it to work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. But how do I work out which discs are worth what value?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I'm not sure, but there will be some logic somewhere. Maybe if you look around the workshop you'll find some clues?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have a look.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oh, I've been rooting through my drawers and I've found a few more discs that you might find useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'm sure they'll be very useful.

sections.title: Attempting to unlock the machine without the correct disc
Attempting to accept an exchange without a disc in the exchanger
Attempting to cancel an exchange without a disc in the exchanger
Attempting to exchange without enough discs in the exchanger
Attempting to insert a disc in the exchanger without one in hand
Attempting to insert a disc in the exchanger after previewing an exchange
Attempting to accept an exchange without previewing the exchange
Attempting to insert a disc in the machine without one in hand
Attempting to activate the machine without the correct discs
Talking to Brimstail after unlocking the machine's front panel

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have opened some sort of control panel.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I wonder how it works? a similar way to the locking mechanism I'd guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There seems to be more than one slot. Maybe I need more than one disc?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huzzah! It's working!

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well done [player name]! I knew you'd work it out. I'll make a mage out of you yet...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Wait... What's happening... Eek!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on earth!?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Izzie! No! There's something very wrong going on here...

sections.title: Attempting to operate the machine after it activates
Talking to Brimstail after activating the machine

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew! I've got that machine working now. What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Well, it seems that Izzie was not so cute after all! How awful! How mysterious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this was an illusion the Arposandrans went to so much effort to conceal? A pet. That wasn't cute enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you find this one?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Izzie just wandered into my cave. He was cute and friendly so I kept him.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: He seemed very curious, and used to watch me do my researches. I liked the companionship – research is lonely work...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So he's been keeping an eye on you while you do important magical research...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I suppose it has. I hadn't thought of it like that. I'm sure I've seen others of these creatures around the place...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: I think King Narnode has one, and I think I saw one by the gates. And I'm sure I've seen a few more wandering around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In other words all places to keep an eye on what's going in the Stronghold...

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Oh my goodness. I think I'm beginning to understand where you are going with this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, they must be spies. They are watching us with those creepy red eyes. And they must have told someone that we were working on the machine.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So that horrible thing was listening to us all along. You'd better tell the King what's going on.

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: So this horrible thing is listening to us right now... Aargh. Get rid of it! Don't forget to tell the king what's going on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get on it now.

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail after killing his evil creature

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do about these creatures?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Have you talked to the King yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get on it now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day sire, I take it you've noticed that cute creature you had in here is looking rather different now.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Why yes! Do you know what's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I do. It's a long story but the short version is this. The Arposandran gnomes have planted these creatures as spies to watch what's going on in the Stronghold.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: What!? That's outrageous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have just reactivated a machine that was built by Oaknock that dispels illusions, and now these creatures true forms are revealed.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Glouphrie! Now we know what he has been up to all these years. Gah! Never trust a Mage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I wouldn't go as far as that – it is only through Brimstail's and my researches that we have established this is going on.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Harumph! Well, all of these creatures must be removed, we cannot allow the Arposandrans to spy on us any more. How many of these things are there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know sire.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Well find them all and dispatch them now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm right on it.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Have you found all of those spies yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I'm still looking for them.

sections.title: Talking to Brimstail after killing all evil creatures

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Brimstail
lines.text: That's a relief. Well, that's all good work, but I think you'd better inform the King now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get on it now.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Have you found all of those spies yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. There were six in total.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Thank goodness. Thank you for your help. Brimstail also tells me that you helped him a lot finding out what was going on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help? I did all of the hard work!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Aha! Getting others to do the donkey work seems to be a skill that Mages develop well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what will you do now?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Well, I have many things to think about. Why are the Arposandrans spying on us, and what are they planning?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's certainly worrying, and whoever sabotaged the machine couldn't have been far from here. Are there gnomes here who are also spies?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Indeed, that is another thing that worries me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you find out more I'd be willing to help investigate.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: That may well be useful. Thank you – I may well call for your help again.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Oh, before you leave – I have something you might like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It was passed down to me from King to King. It belonged to Oaknock. He proclaimed that only a hero of the gnomes should have it. I think you count as a hero. Also since you have been looking into Oaknock you

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: might be able to work out what it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm I wonder what it does?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Well, I'm sure that your magical research skills will help you there. Good luck!
